behaviors.description: The wait time between retries doubles with each consecutive failure, up to a configured maximum.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is retry/backoff math in one local policy component. The behavior text is purely about delay calculation ("doubles ... up to a configured maximum"), so no module handoff, OS, network, or subprocess is required. Not Integration because no cross-module invariant is needed; not System because no real infra is required.
behaviors.test_description: Test a pure `computeBackoff(attempt, baseDelay, maxDelay)` (or equivalent policy method) with consecutive attempt numbers and assert exponential growth plus cap clamping.

behaviors.description: The retry logic correctly identifies a rate-limit response and computes the appropriate wait time before the next attempt.
behaviors.justification: This failure appears at a local module boundary: response classification + retry scheduler/backoff policy wiring. Behavior text includes both identifying a rate-limit response and computing wait, which is a handoff invariant across components. Not System: a stubbed HTTP response/status+headers reproduces this exactly. Not Agentic/Workflow: no AI decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Wire `call()` with a fake transport returning a 429 (and retry metadata such as `Retry-After` if supported) and a fake clock; assert retry classifier marks it retryable and scheduler uses the expected delay.

behaviors.description: After the wait, the client actually resends the original request and returns the successful result to the caller transparently.
behaviors.justification: Requires orchestration across timer/backoff, request replay, and result propagation to caller. This is more than one function, but still in-process. Not System because behavior text does not require real network timing; fake timers + stub transport can expose resend/transparent return bugs. Not Agentic/Workflow: no model output dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Use fake clock and scripted transport: first response is rate-limited, second succeeds. Assert same request payload is reissued after advancing clock by computed delay, and `call()` resolves with second response body without exposing retry internals.

behaviors.description: After the configured maximum number of retries, the client returns a typed `QuotaExhaustedError` rather than a generic error.
behaviors.justification: Needs retry-loop state + terminal error mapping interaction. Behavior text explicitly ties retry exhaustion to typed error surface, which is a cross-component contract at public `call()` boundary. Not System: deterministic stubbed failures reproduce exhaustion path. Not Agentic/Workflow: no AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Configure max retries N, stub transport to return rate-limit failures for N+1 attempts, run `call()`, and assert rejection is `instanceof QuotaExhaustedError` with expected metadata, not a generic transport error.

behaviors.description: The full retry chain — initial call, backoff retries, quota tracking — terminates correctly and does not loop indefinitely before surfacing the error.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is explicitly end-to-end within library internals (retry chain + quota tracking), so multiple modules must be wired. Not Unit because infinite-loop risk emerges from interaction of counters/scheduler/quota state. Not System because fake timers and deterministic stub responses can prove termination and bounded attempts without real OS/network.
behaviors.test_description: Run `call()` with fake clock and always-rate-limited stub transport; assert total attempts are bounded by configured limits/quota state, promise settles (rejects) within bounded virtual time, and no extra scheduled retries remain.

behaviors.description: Requests issued concurrently from multiple async callers in the same process all draw from the same shared quota bucket.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is shared in-process state across concurrent callers, i.e., module interaction between caller-facing client instances/tasks and quota bucket implementation. Not Unit: single-function tests miss race/coordination bugs. Not System: behavior text scopes to "same process" and can be reproduced with in-process concurrency primitives and stub transport.
behaviors.test_description: Create multiple concurrent `call()` invocations against one library process with a shared quota manager, using controllable async barriers; assert combined consumed tokens/requests respect one shared bucket rather than per-caller independent limits.

behaviors.description: When multiple requests are waiting to be sent, they are dispatched in the order they were originally submitted.
behaviors.justification: This is queueing/dispatcher ordering across async coordination + send path, a cross-module invariant not isolated to one function. Not System: FIFO dispatch correctness can be validated with in-process queue and stub transport; no real network/OS behavior required by text. Not Agentic/Workflow: no model steps.
behaviors.test_description: Enqueue several `call()` requests while dispatch is blocked (quota exhausted or gate held), then release capacity and assert transport send order matches submission order exactly.
